edges.description: Loss of inhibitory SMAD6 increases the osteogenic drive within cranial suture developmental programs.
intermediate_mechanisms: impaired inhibitory feedback on BMP signaling
edges.source: Germline SMAD6 loss with impaired BMP restraint
edges.target: Excess BMP signaling in cranial suture mesenchyme

edges.description: Excess BMP signaling pushes suture mesenchyme toward premature osteoblast maturation and bone deposition.
edges.source: Excess BMP signaling in cranial suture mesenchyme
edges.target: Accelerated osteoblast differentiation and suture ossification

edges.description: Early osteogenic closure produces the characteristic midline synostosis phenotype.
edges.source: Accelerated osteoblast differentiation and suture ossification

edges.description: Premature cranial suture fusion can restrict cranial vault growth and lead to neurologic risk from raised intracranial pressure.
intermediate_mechanisms: cranial vault growth restriction after premature suture fusion
edges.source: Accelerated osteoblast differentiation and suture ossification
edges.target: Cranial vault growth restriction and raised intracranial pressure risk
Germline SMAD6 loss with impaired BMP restraint

nodes.description: The immediate cranial mechanism is premature osteogenic maturation and ossification at cranial sutures, with strongest enrichment in midline sutures.
nodes.id: Accelerated osteoblast differentiation and suture ossification

evidence.reference_title: SMAD6 variants in craniosynostosis: genotype and phenotype evaluation.
evidence.snippet: Pathogenic SMAD6 variants substantially increase the risk of both nonsyndromic and syndromic presentations of craniosynostosis, especially metopic synostosis.

nodes.description: Premature suture fusion can constrain cranial vault expansion and create a risk of raised intracranial pressure, particularly in multisuture or postoperative contexts.
nodes.id: Cranial vault growth restriction and raised intracranial pressure risk

evidence.reference_title: SMAD6 variants in craniosynostosis: genotype and phenotype evaluation.
evidence.snippet: Although based on small numbers, the observation that three subjects developed raised ICP following their primary surgical procedure suggests that long-term postoperative follow-up of these patients is important for optimal management.

nodes.description: Failure of SMAD6-mediated negative feedback permits excessive BMP-driven osteogenic signaling in the mesenchymal compartment that normally preserves suture patency.
nodes.id: Excess BMP signaling in cranial suture mesenchyme

evidence.snippet: Until now, SMAD6-deficiency has been associated with three distinctive human congenital conditions, i.e., congenital heart diseases, including left ventricular obstruction and conotruncal defects, craniosynostosis and radioulnar synostosis.

nodes.description: Heterozygous deleterious SMAD6 variants reduce inhibitory control of BMP signaling, forming the shared upstream lesion of the craniosynostosis branch.
nodes.id: Germline SMAD6 loss with impaired BMP restraint

evidence.snippet: SMAD6 encodes an intracellular inhibitor of the bone morphogenetic protein (BMP) signalling pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Smad6 is essential to limit BMP signaling during cartilage development.
evidence.snippet: Overall, our results show that Smad6 is required to limit BMP signaling during endochondral bone formation.

nodes.description: Truncating SMAD6 variant reported in a metopic synostosis proband.
Truncating SMAD6 variant reported in a sagittal craniosynostosis proband.

evidence.reference_title: Two locus inheritance of non-syndromic midline craniosynostosis via rare SMAD6 and common BMP2 alleles.
evidence.snippet: Thirteen probands (7%) had damaging de novo or rare transmitted mutations in SMAD6, an inhibitor of BMP - induced osteoblast differentiation (p<10-20).

evidence.reference_title: SMAD6 variants in craniosynostosis: genotype and phenotype evaluation.
evidence.snippet: Pathogenic SMAD6 variants substantially increase the risk of both nonsyndromic and syndromic presentations of craniosynostosis, especially metopic synostosis.
